title: US Accuses China of Industrial-Scale AI Theft
summary: The White House has issued a memo accusing foreign entities, primarily based in China, of conducting industrial-scale campaigns to steal US artificial intelligence (AI) technology. Michael Kratsios, Director of Science and Technology Policy, stated that these entities use a technique called 'distilling' to copy AI models. Distillation involves operating thousands of accounts to appear as normal users, then attempting to 'jailbreak' the AI to extract proprietary information. The memo outlines four unspecified actions to combat this exploitation. A representative from China's US embassy countered the accusations, claiming China's AI development is a result of its own efforts and international cooperation, and criticized US suppression of Chinese companies. Major AI firms like OpenAI and Anthropic have reported similar distillation activities. The memo emphasizes that as detection methods improve, foreign entities relying on stolen technology will produce unreliable models.

keywords.explanation: a method of copying AI by using many fake accounts to extract information
tricking an AI into revealing hidden information or bypassing restrictions
secret or private data owned by a company
very large, like a factory production line

questions.question: What technique does the White House say Chinese entities use to steal AI?
What did China's embassy representative say?
Which AI companies have reported distillation activity?
How many actions did the White House outline to stop the theft?
What does Kratsios say about the reliability of stolen models?
options: They are as good as originals

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the memo?
options: To accuse foreign entities of theft
questions.correct_answer: To accuse foreign entities of theft

background_read: The article discusses US-China tensions over AI technology. AI is a strategic field for both countries. The US leads in AI innovation but faces competition from China. Distillation is a known method of copying AI models. The memo reflects US efforts to protect intellectual property.
Article_Structure: Main Points: The White House accuses Chinese entities of industrial-scale AI theft via distillation. Purpose: To alert US AI firms and announce actions to protect technology. Evidence Evaluation: The memo cites new information but provides no specific evidence; major AI companies confirm similar activities. Author Credibility: Michael Kratsios is the Director of Science and Technology Policy, a high-ranking official. Methodology: The memo is a policy statement, not a research document; the claims are based on internal information.

perspectives.description: Views Chinese AI theft as a threat to national security and economic competitiveness.
Rejects accusations, highlights China's own innovation, and criticizes US suppression.
